decks.notes: This deck focuses on using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures to control the game and grind out victories. It utilizes cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Abrupt Decay to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while deploying threats like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to pressure the opponent. With a solid mana base and a mixture of removal, disruption, and threats, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast its opponent in a grindy, attrition-based matchup.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang, Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and control spells. The key strategy is to utilize cards like Fleeting Spirit and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to generate value and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Monument to Endurance provides protection for your creatures while Thoughtseize and Duress help to disrupt the opponent's hand. Vanishing Verse is included for removal purposes, and Bitter Triumph can serve as a finisher. The deck aims to create a strong board presence and control the game until victory is achieved.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize a mix of powerful legends to create a fast and aggressive playstyle. By ramping up with mana accelerators like Mox Amber and Cavern of Souls, the deck aims to quickly bring out strong legendary creatures like Arachne, Rona, and Jirina to overwhelm the opponent. With support cards like Relic of Legends and Plaza of Heroes, the deck can boost these legends even further, allowing for a swift and dominating victory on the battlefield.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to establish a fast and aggressive board presence by utilizing low-cost creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter, Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger. With spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to remove threats and push damage through, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly. By utilizing cards like Academic Dispute, Boomerang Basics, and Stormchaser's Talent for card advantage and tempo plays, the deck can maintain its aggression while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck's mana base supports its spells with Shivan Reef, Riverpyre Verge, Spirebluff Canal, and Steam Vents.

This control deck revolves around using Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a powerful card advantage engine, supported by Bring to Light to find key cards. The deck utilizes a variety of lands, including Triomes and Verge lands, to fix mana and ensure consistency. The deck also includes Leyline Binding to disrupt opponents' strategies and powerful spells like Omnath, Locus of Creation and Lightning Helix to control the board and gain value. With a versatile toolbox of cards and multiple win conditions, this deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents in a grindy game.

The main focus of this deck is to maintain control over the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan using removal spells and discard effects. The deck also aims to generate card advantage through the use of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion, which allows for additional value from playing permanents with enter-the-battlefield effects. The deck features a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful threats like Elder Gargaroth and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to dominate the battlefield and outvalue the opponent in a long game.

The main concept of this aggro Red Deck Wins pioneer deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures and burn spells. It focuses on dealing direct damage to the opponent while maintaining board presence with efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage. The inclusion of cards like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Reckless Rage helps control the board while pushing through damage. The deck is designed to quickly close out games before the opponent has a chance to stabilize, making it a powerful and relentless aggressive deck in the pioneer format.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The deck focuses on early game pressure with cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, while also having the ability to clear the way with removal spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck aims to control the battlefield and keep the pressure up until the opponent is defeated. It also utilizes some tempo plays with cards like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and tokens. It includes cards that support this strategy such as Bitter Triumph, Cache Grab, and Esika's Chariot. Additionally, it includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Duress to control the game and protect the combo. The deck also features various lands to ensure consistency and mana fixing. Overall, the goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful and aggressive strategy.

The deck revolves around assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that can overwhelm opponents. The deck includes support cards like Temple Garden and Formidable Speaker to help set up the combo efficiently. It also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize to control the game and protect the combo. The goal is to generate value through efficient card interactions and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

The key concept of this Gruul Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creatures, utilizing burn spells and removal to clear the way for direct damage. The deck aims to establish a board presence early on with efficient creatures like Questing Druid and Monastery Swiftspear, while also maintaining tempo with cards like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. By combining creature aggression with burn spells and removal, this deck seeks to close out games quickly and efficiently by applying constant pressure on the opponent.

This deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang Parhelion's combo potential with cards like Fleeting Spirit, Parhelion II, and Greasefang, Okiba Boss to generate powerful board states and overwhelming advantages. The deck includes a mix of disruption, removal, and protection to ensure the combo pieces can come together smoothly and efficiently. The strategy revolves around setting up the combo pieces while controlling the opponent's resources and slowly building towards a game-winning position with a powerful engine fueled by the synergy between the cards in the deck.

The main concept of this deck is to use the Greasefang Parhelion combo to generate powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. By utilizing cards like Fleeting Spirit, Monument to Endurance, and Guardian of New Benalia, the deck aims to set up a strong defensive strategy while also building towards the combo. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board and disrupt their game plan. The deck focuses on efficiently ramping up with mana sources like Godless Shrine and Concealed Courtyard to ensure that the combo can be executed quickly and effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures and burn spells. By using efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage along with burn spells like Lightning Strike and Burst Lightning, the deck aims to deal consistent damage to the opponent early in the game. Cards like Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage provide additional removal and damage, while cards like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins offer late-game reach. Overall, the deck seeks to win through fast and relentless aggression.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to protect and support the combo pieces. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board while cards like Guardian of New Benalia and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional support. The deck uses a mix of lands to fix mana and provide utility, allowing for a consistent and powerful combo strategy that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The key strategy is to set up the combo pieces and protect them until they can be used to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient spells and creatures to deal damage quickly and aggressively to the opponent. By combining cards that boost spell damage and provide creature evasion, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to set up their own game plan. The strategy revolves around utilizing instants and sorceries to control the board while building up a creature presence that can quickly close out the game.
